heading: Airplane parts and accessories: civilian flying schools
label: 7628 Airplane parts and accessories: civilian flying schools
text: The Secretary of the Army may sell, to civilian flying schools at which personnel of the Department of the Army or the Department of the Air Force are receiving flight training under contracts requiring these schools to maintain and repair airplanes of the Army furnished to them for flight training, the spare parts and accessories needed for those repairs.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)462810:298c., Title I (proviso under “Air Corps”), .Feb. 12, 1940, ch. 2754 Stat. 25The words “under the provisions of the , ” are omitted as obsolete, since training formerly performed under that act is now performed under . The words “personnel of the Department of the Army or the Department of the Air Force” are substituted for the words “flying cadets”, since the authority is reciprocal, and to conform to . The words “flying cadet” are omitted as obsolete. 10:298c (last 28 words) is omitted to reflect Title IV of the National Security Act of 1947, as amended (), which authorized the Secretary of Defense to prescribe regulations governing the use and sale of certain inventories at cost, including applicable administrative expenses. (See opinion of the Assistant General Counsel (Fiscal Matters) of the Office of the Secretary of Defense, .)Act of Apr. 3, 1939, ch. 3553 Stat. 555section 4301 of this titlesection 4656 of this title63 Stat. 585January 4, 1955

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019
